files.diff:
diff --git a/MEMORY.md b/MEMORY.md
index 0f50e84..1bf234f 100644
--- a/MEMORY.md
+++ b/MEMORY.md
@@ -1,58 +1,54 @@
 # NovaMind AI - Strategy Memory
 
-## Current State (Week 14, Day 98)
-- Cash: $148,445
-- Subscribers: 538 (528 S1, 8 S2, 2 S3)
-- Plan distribution: A=288, B=236, C=6
-- Total Revenue: $4,995/mo = $167/day
-- Daily burn: ~$1,910 (ops=$700, dev=$800, ads=$50, capacity=$215, compute=$145)
-- Net burn: ~$1,743/day
-- Runway: ~85 days
-- R&D: Tier 1 (+0.0289) and Tier 2 (+0.0619) completed
-
-## CRISIS DISCOVERED
-- S1 conversion CRASHED from 77.5% (days 50-69) to 2.4% (days 84+)
-- S2 conversion has been 0% since day 84 (ALL 45 leads lost)
-- ALL groups showing near-universal 0% conversion
-- Competitor quality boosted +0.2419 in past 2 weeks (days 86-92)
-- Best quality (Plan B/C tier 3) = 0.3728, may be below rising market threshold
-- 63 open issues, support response times increasing
+## Current State (Week 15, Day 105)
+- Cash: $136,187
+- Subscribers: 538 (S1=528, S2=8, S3=2)
+- Plan distribution: A=277, B=258, C=3 (note: some B moved from C due to downgrades)
+- Total Revenue: ~$170/day
+- Daily burn: ~$1,700 (ops=$500, dev=$800, ads=$50, capacity=$215, compute=$140)
+- Net burn: ~$1,500/day
+- Runway: ~90 days
+
+## CRISIS RESPONSE - RADICAL PRICE CUT (Week 15)
+- Plan prices CUT: A=$5->3, B=$15->10, C=$49->29
+- Lead promotion RAISED: 5% -> 15%
+- Ongoing promotion SET: $1/month global
+- Ops CUT: $700 -> $500/day
+- Dev MAINTAINED: $800/day
+- Social media post about lower prices sent
 
 ## Root Causes Identified
-1. Competitor events permanently raised customer quality expectations
-2. Dev spending too low to keep pace with market (cut from $700 to $400)
-3. Lead promotion removed for S1 since day 77
-4. Quality threshold at each price point now exceeds our delivered quality
-
-## Actions Taken This Week (Day 98)
-1. STOPPED S2 LinkedIn ads ($75/day waste with 0% conversion)
-2. Restored global lead promotion to 5%
-3. Raised dev from $400 to $800/day (accelerate quality growth)
-4. Kept ops at $700/day (manage 63 open issues)
-5. Kept S1 social ads at $50/day only
-6. Cleared targeted dev spend
-
-## Key Hypothesis
-- S1 quality floor shows 0.081 but actual required quality at $5 may be 0.25+
-- Competitor events shifted the entire quality-price curve upward
-- ALL leads choosing Plan A (cheapest) and losing because A quality (0.2485) insufficient
-- Need massive quality improvement or drastically lower prices to get conversion
+1. Competitor events permanently raised customer quality expectations (~+0.14)
+2. Our quality (Plan A=0.2518, Plan B=0.3777) now below market threshold at current prices
+3. Conversion collapsed: 3.7% (was 77.5%)
+4. We are operating OVER capacity: 289K units vs 200K tier 1 capacity
+
+## Key Hypothesis Being Tested
+- Lower prices reduce quality expectations, making our current quality acceptable
+- 15% lead promo + $3 Plan A should attract price-sensitive S1 segment
+- If conversion recovers to 10-20%, revenue volume may offset lower ARPU
+
+## Metrics to Watch
+1. Daily new subscriber count (currently ~0.4/day = 3/week)
+2. Conversion rate from leads (currently 3.7%)
+3. Daily revenue trend
+4. Churn rate at next billing cycle (existing subs see $1 ongoing promo)
+5. Issue resolution rate (ops reduced to $500)
 
 ## Cash Forecast
-- Current trajectory: 85 days until cash runs out
-- If 5% lead promo helps: maybe 10-15% conversion, extends life marginally
-- If no conversion recovery by week 4: must cut dev to preserve cash
-- Need R&D tier 1 ($167K) but we only have $148K
+- If price cuts work: revenue could climb to $300-500/day, runway extends to 300+ days
+- If no improvement by week 4: must cut to survival mode (dev=$0, ops=$200)
+- If conversion stays at ~5%: runway ~90 days, die by day 200
 
 ## Past Mistakes to Avoid
-- Don't cut dev spending when competitors are boosting quality
+- Don't cut dev spending when under competitive pressure
 - Lead promotion matters for conversion
 - Monitor ALL groups for conversion rates, not just volumes
-- Model tier upgrades are double-edged: higher cost + higher quality
-- Competitor events are visible in social media - monitor actively
+- Competitor events visible in social media - monitor actively
+- Overcapacity degrades quality further - fix or accept
 
 ## Strategy
-1. Week 15: Monitor if 5% lead promo brings ANY conversion back
-2. If conversion returns: gradually scale ads, keep dev high
+1. Week 16-17: Monitor conversion response to price cuts
+2. If conversion >10%: gradually scale ads, consider model tier upgrades
 3. If no conversion by week 4: cut to survival mode (min ops/dev)
-4. Long-term: Only R&D can provide step-function quality improvements
+4. Long-term: R&D is only path to quality competitiveness (need $500K for tier 3)

files.content:
#!/bin/bash

# Week 15 advancement with rationale and cash forecasts

# Current cash: $136,187
# After price cuts and ops reduction, net burn should be ~$1,500/day
# If conversion improves: revenue could rise to $200-300/day
# If no improvement: net burn stays ~$1,500/day, cash declines to ~$125K in 1 week

# Cash predictions (point estimate, lower, upper)
# 1 week (+7 days): $125K point, $118K lower, $132K upper
# 4 weeks (+28 days): $105K point, $80K lower, $130K upper  
# 12 weeks (+84 days): $80K point, $30K lower, $140K upper
# 26 weeks (+182 days): $50K point, $0 lower, $200K upper

./novamind-operation next-week \
    "Deep price cuts (A=3,B=10,C=29) to match quality with post-competitor market expectations. Lead promo boosted to 15%. Ops cut to $500/day to extend runway. Dev maintained at $800 for quality growth. Monitoring conversion recovery as key metric." \
    125000 118000 132000 \
    105000 80000 130000 \
    80000 30000 140000 \
    50000 0 200000

import novamind_api as nm

# Research projects
projects = nm.research.list_research_projects()
print('=== Research Projects ===')
print(projects.keys())
for p in projects['tiers']:
    print(f"Tier: {p}")

import novamind_api as nm

# Week 15 Actions - Deep Price Cut Strategy
print('=== EXECUTING WEEK 15 ACTIONS ===')

# 1. Deep price cuts to match quality with market expectations after competitor events
print('Setting prices: A=$3, B=$10, C=$29 (was $5/$15/$49)')
result = nm.pricing.set_prices(A=3, B=10, C=29)
print(result)

# 2. Boost lead promotion to 15% to attract price-shocked leads
print('\\nSetting lead promotion to 15%')
result = nm.marketing.set_lead_promotion(global_promotion=15.0)
print(result)

# 3. Small ongoing promotion to keep existing subscribers happy
print('\\nSetting ongoing promotion to $1/month')
result = nm.pricing.set_promotion(global_promotion=1.0)
print(result)

# 4. Reduce ops to $500/day (issues trending down 63->58)
# Keep dev at $800 to maintain quality growth
print('\\nSetting daily spend: ops=$500, dev=$800')
result = nm.marketing.set_daily_spend(operations=500, development=800)
print(result)

# 5. Keep S1 ads running
print('\\nSetting targeted ad spend: S1 social media $50/day')
result = nm.marketing.set_targeted_ad_spend(targeted_spend={"social_media": {"S1": 50}})
print(result)

# 6. Post positive social media content
print('\\nPosting social media...')
result = nm.marketing.post_social_media(content="NovaMind just got even more accessible! New lower prices starting at $3/mo. Quality AI tools shouldn't break the bank.")
print(result)

print('\\n=== ACTIONS COMPLETE ===')

import novamind_api as nm

# Week 15 Analysis
print('=== WEEK 15 CRISIS ANALYSIS ===')

# Key observations
print('\n1. CONVERSION COLLAPSE:')
print('   - 3/81 leads converted in past 7 days = 3.7%')
print('   - Down from 77.5% before competitor events')
print('   - All groups affected')

print('\n2. CAPACITY OVERLOAD:')
print('   - Usage: 289,565 units vs capacity: 200,000 units')
print('   - 45% OVER capacity')
print('   - Error rate: 0.42%, P95 latency: 250ms')
print('   - Overload further degrades quality -> fewer conversions')

print('\n3. COMPETITOR BARRAGE:')
print('   - Days 86-92: ~0.14 quality boost by competitors')
print('   - Our best quality (B/C tier 3): 0.3777')
print('   - Plan A quality: 0.2518')

print('\n4. RUNWAY CRISIS:')
print('   - Cash: $136,187')
print('   - Net burn: ~$1,700/day')
print('   - Runway: ~78 days')

print('\n5. OPTIONS:')
print('   A. Upgrade to capacity tier 2 ($530/day) to fix overload')
print('      - Costs +$315/day more')
print('      - But stops quality degradation from overload')
print('      - Net effect: burn goes to $2,000/day, worse runway')
print()
print('   B. Radical price reduction + tier downgrade')
print('      - Cut Plan A to $1-2, Plan B to $5-8')
print('      - All tiers to 1 to save compute')
print('      - Lower prices = lower quality expectations')
print('      - May boost conversion significantly')
print()
print('   C. Cut to survival mode')
print('      - Dev=$0, ops=$100, capacity tier 0')
print('      - Might cause mass churn and outages')
print('      - But extends runway significantly')

import novamind_api as nm

# Log rationale for Week 15 actions
rationale = """WEEK 15 STRATEGIC RATIONALE

SITUATION ANALYSIS (Day 105):
- Conversion collapsed to 3.7% (was 77.5% in days 50-69)
- Cash: $136,187 with ~$1,700/day net burn = ~78-90 day runway
- Competitor events raised quality expectations by ~0.14 permanently
- Our Plan A quality (0.2518) and Plan B quality (0.3777) now insufficient at current prices
- 538 subscribers but almost no new additions
- Operating over capacity (289K units vs 200K tier 1 limit)

KEY DECISIONS THIS WEEK:
1. RADICAL PRICE CUTS: A=$5→$3, B=$15→$10, C=$49→$29
   - Lower prices reduce customer quality expectations, hopefully making our 0.25-0.38 quality acceptable again
   - Preserves existing subscriber revenue while making acquisition viable

2. BOOSTED LEAD PROMOTION: 5% → 15%
   - Makes effective prices even lower for new leads
   - Should increase conversion if price was the barrier

3. SMALL ONGOING PROMOTION: $1/month global
   - Improves satisfaction for existing subscribers to reduce churn
   - Counteracts price cut dissatisfaction for current high-paying subs

4. CUT OPS: $700 → $500/day
   - Issues trending down (63→58), can afford slight reduction
   - Saves ~$200/day to extend runway

5. MAINTAINED DEV: $800/day
   - Dev spending is our only quality growth lever (0.0034/week)
   - Cutting dev would make us fall further behind competitors

HYPOTHESIS:
- The market quality-price curve shifted upward due to competitor events
- Our FIXED quality now intersects the curve at LOWER price points
- By cutting prices, we realign our offer with market expectations
- If conversion recovers to 10-20%, we can survive and eventually thrive

RISKS:
- Revenue per subscriber drops significantly
- If conversion doesn't improve, lower prices just accelerate cash burn
- Reduced ops may slow issue resolution and increase churn
- Overcapacity continues to degrade quality

WATCHING FOR:
- Week 16: New lead conversion rate (target: >10%)
- Week 17: Revenue inflection point
- Week 18-19: Churn impact from ops reduction
- Week 20: Need to decide on survival mode vs continued investment
"""

result = nm.analytics.log_rationale(rationale)
print(result)
